---
title: "caladaptR"
subtitle: "Native R Access to Cal-Adapt Climate Projections"
---

`caladaptR` reads downscaled CMIP6 climate data from California's
[Cal-Adapt Analytics Engine](https://analytics.cal-adapt.org/) directly into R.
No Python, no API key, no authentication -- just GDAL reading Zarr stores
from AWS S3.

## Available Data

| Dataset | Resolution | Temporal | GCMs | SSPs | Period |
|---------|-----------|----------|------|------|--------|
| WRF CMIP6 (WUS-D3) | 45 / 9 / 3 km | Hourly | 8 models | SSP3-7.0 (all); SSP2-4.5, SSP5-8.5 (CESM2) | 1980--2100 |
| LOCA2-Hybrid | 3 km | Daily | 15 models | SSP2-4.5, SSP3-7.0, SSP5-8.5 | 1950--2100 |

## Quick Start

```r
library(caladaptR)

# What's available?
ca_models(activity = "WRF")
ca_variables(activity = "WRF", timescale = "1hr")

# Read hourly temperature, 45 km grid, one day
t2 <- ca_fetch("t2", model = "CESM2", scenario = "ssp370",
               n_timesteps = 24)

# Extract time series at a point
ts <- ca_fetch_point("t2", model = "CESM2", scenario = "ssp370",
                     lon = -119.77, lat = 36.75,
                     n_timesteps = 24)
```

## Guides

| Vignette | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Getting Started](vignettes/getting_started.qmd) | Catalog queries, fetching gridded data, point extraction, visualization |
| [SIPNET Met Drivers](vignettes/sipnet_met_drivers.qmd) | Build CF-standard NetCDF met drivers for SIPNET from WRF projections |

## Installation

```r
remotes::install_github("lebauerapproach/caladaptR")
```

## License

BSD 3-Clause
